hero-tagline: Four years turning regulatory and business complexity into delivered systems. I own the work end to end, from requirements and stakeholder alignment to production and steering. The next step is leading it.
about-p1: I am a data and business analyst working at the interface between business teams and information systems, across regulated banking and international industrial environments. Over four years I have consistently owned delivery rather than just contributing to it: equally comfortable designing the data pipeline and steering the project that ships it.
about-p2: I run engagements end to end. Requirements gathering and workshop facilitation, functional specifications, backlog ownership, UAT coordination, production hand-off, and steering committee reporting. I make trade-offs explicit, set the quality bar for deliverables, and translate fluently between technical teams and non-technical stakeholders. This is how a delivery lead and a product owner operate, and it is how I already work.
about-p3: I am currently on a mission within the automotive-leasing subsidiary of a major European banking group, where I own the regulatory delivery on Basel III Standard Approach frameworks, NDoD reporting, and credit risk segmentation. I arbitrate between Risk, Finance, and Compliance, drive the backlog from specification to production, and report delivery and risk status to the steering committee.
about-p4: Engineering degree from Télécom Saint-Étienne, FRM certification in progress (GARP). My objective is to move from owning individual delivery to leading it: product ownership and data delivery leadership, full remote, for clients and teams across Western Europe in Finance, Risk, or regulatory data.
experience-1-title: Data & Business Analyst · Risk & Finance IT
experience-1-employer: Solutec, on mission at the automotive-leasing subsidiary of a major European banking group
experience-1-bullet1: Owned the design and delivery of an Oracle SQL processing engine covering default-event detection, cure identification, and prudential portfolio segmentation. Translated complex regulatory logic (NDoD, EBA / Basel III / CRR) into auditable data transformations, through to production.
experience-1-bullet2: Led requirements across Risk, Finance, and Compliance, and authored the functional specifications (business rules, data dictionaries, ETL flows, test cases) that became the team's reference.
experience-1-bullet3: Facilitated scoping and prioritisation workshops as MOA / MOE liaison, owned the Jira backlog, and drove user stories from specification to production.
experience-1-bullet4: Coordinated UAT campaigns end to end, owning anomaly traceability and the correction loop.
experience-1-bullet5: Built credit-risk KPI monitoring and data-quality controls, and reported delivery and risk status to the steering committee.
experience-2-employer: Lennox EMEA, listed industrial group, EMEA scope
experience-2-bullet1: Owned an end-to-end BI migration (legacy to Power BI / SSRS) across European entities: requirements, architecture, deployment, and user training, lifting cross-department reporting efficiency by 30%.
experience-2-bullet2: Built and shipped an XGBoost forecasting model into the S&OP process, and presented results and recommendations directly to business stakeholders, improving forecast reliability by 25%.
experience-2-bullet3: Designed Python / SQL pipelines automating multi-entity financial reporting flows.
experience-2-bullet4: Built cost and margin analytics that drove the quarterly removal of 10+ unprofitable projects, alongside dynamic production, sales, and finance dashboards.
projects-intro: Self-directed projects where I owned the full cycle: scoping, architecture, build, and delivery. Each one reflects how I make technical decisions and ship.
project-1-desc: A production-grade PL/SQL credit scoring engine on Oracle XE, simulating a synthetic automotive-leasing portfolio. I architected the separation between auditable business logic in SQL and application code, implemented the Basel III Standard Approach for capital requirement calculation (risk-weight assignment, exposure segmentation, RWA computation), and shipped stored procedures, control tables, and reconciliation views.
project-2-desc: A Power BI dashboard on public EBA data, comparing major European banks across key prudential ratios (CET1, Tier 1, LCR, NSFR). Built to demonstrate regulatory data literacy and visual analytics in a Finance and Risk context.
project-3-desc: An end-to-end credit scoring model built with XGBoost on a synthetic loan portfolio, with full SHAP explainability for individual predictions. Deployed as an interactive Streamlit application: input borrower characteristics, receive a risk score and a breakdown of contributing factors. Built to bridge ML modelling with regulatory explainability requirements.
colophon-set-in: Set in Newsreader, Inter & IBM Plex Mono
